metadata.seed: Yeah. I was at a party, and I was kind of hesitant about going, and then I went and I realized, like, I just can't talk. Like, I don't like meeting new people. I never know what to say. And then it's, like, an awkward silence, and then I just feel like I don't want to be there anymore.

patient.query: Yeah. I was at a party, and I was kind of hesitant about going, and then I went and I realized, like, I just can't talk. Like, I don't like meeting new people. patient.query: Yeah, it’s just this heavy feeling, you know? I start to overthink everything—like, maybe I said something wrong or I’m just not fun to talk to. It feels like everyone's judging me, and I just want to disappear. It’s like everything gets so loud in my head, and all I can focus on is the silence.

automatic_thoughts: I’m just not fun to talk to

llm_response.response:
Okay, I hear you. It sounds incredibly difficult to be carrying this "heavy feeling" and to have those thoughts swirling around – wondering if you said the wrong thing, feeling like you're not fun to be around, and worrying about what others think. It makes complete sense that you'd want to disappear when you're experiencing all of that. That sense of everything getting loud in your head, and then focusing on the silence, paints a really vivid picture of how overwhelming this feels.

It sounds like you're caught in a cycle of self-doubt and worry. Can you tell me a little more about what happens *right before* you start to feel this way and have these thoughts?

patient.query: Yeah, it usually hits me out of nowhere, like I’m just hanging out with friends or even just at home, and then suddenly I’m thinking about something I said or did in the past. It feels like my mind just grabs onto those moments and won’t let go. I start to feel this tightness in my chest, and then the worries flood in, making it hard to think about anything else. It's like I can't shake off the feeling that I'm not good enough, you know?

patient.query: It’s just... sometimes I compare myself to others, like when I see them laughing and having a good time, and I feel so out of place. I start questioning if I’m interesting enough or if people really want me around. I think about times when I messed up or didn’t fit in, and it’s like those moments just replay in my mind. It makes me feel really small and alone.

It feels like I'm standing on the outside looking in, and I just get this wave of panic that I won't ever fit in. It almost feels like drowning, just trying to catch my breath while everyone's having

patient.query: I guess I've always felt a bit different. Like, in school, I was never the one who got invited to hang out. I remember feeling so uncomfortable in groups, not knowing what to say or how to join in. So now, when I’m with people, those old feelings come back, and I just... I don't know, I feel like I’m putting on a show trying to keep up. I worry they can see right through me and that I’m just not what they want.
